metadata.title: Health Canada Guidance Document for Clinical Trial Sponsors: Clinical Trial Applications
metadata.notes: Canada-specific CTA/CTD guidance. Existing output-v3 health-canada-requirements.json is a framework document not in standard rule format; this extraction covers detailed procedural/filing rules from the CTA guidance document.

rules.rule_text: Clinical trial sponsors must submit a Clinical Trial Application (CTA) to Health Canada prior to the initiation of a clinical trial, for authorization to sell or import a drug for clinical trial purposes in Canada.
rules.verbatim_quote: clinical trial sponsors must submit a clinical trial application (CTA) to Health Canada for authorization to sell or import a drug for the purpose of a clinical trial.
rules.exceptions: Phase IV studies within the parameters of the approved NOC or DIN do not require a CTA [C.05.006(2)].
cross_references: Food and Drug Regulations Part C Division 5

rules.rule_text: The CTA must be filed prior to the initiation of the trial. CTAs are required for all human clinical trials in Phases I through III, comparative bioavailability studies, and trials of marketed drugs used outside the parameters of the NOC or DIN.
rules.verbatim_quote: The sponsor must file a CTA prior to the initiation of the trial [C.05.005]. CTAs are required for human clinical trials using drugs not authorized for sale in Canada, including clinical trials in Phases I through III of drug development and comparative bioavailability studies
rules.exceptions: Phase IV trials within approved parameters are exempt.

rules.rule_text: The CTA must be composed of three modules in CTD format: Module 1 (Administrative and Clinical Information), Module 2 (Quality/Chemistry and Manufacturing Summaries), and Module 3 (Additional Supporting Quality Information).
rules.verbatim_quote: The CTA is composed of three parts (modules) in accordance with the CTD format: Module 1 - contains administrative and clinical information about the proposed trial; Module 2 - contains Quality (Chemistry and Manufacturing) summaries about the drug product(s) to be used in the proposed trial; and Module 3 - contains additional supporting Quality information.

rules.rule_text: The CTA must be submitted on electronic media, accompanied by a hard copy cover letter, organized per the Non-eCTD Electronic-Only Format guidance.
rules.verbatim_quote: The CTA should be submitted on electronic media, accompanied by a hard copy cover letter, and be organized in accordance with the current electronic specifications: Guidance Document: Preparation of Drug Regulatory Activities in the "Non-eCTD Electronic-Only" Format.

rules.rule_text: Module 1 must include a completed and signed Drug Submission Application Form (HC/SC 3011), including Appendix 3, signed by the Senior Medical or Scientific Officer in Canada and the Senior Executive Officer.
rules.verbatim_quote: A completed and signed Drug Submission Application Form (HC/SC 3011)... including Appendix 3, signed by the Senior Medical or Scientific Officer in Canada and the Senior Executive Officer.
rules.exceptions: For institution/investigator-initiated clinical trials, Appendix 3 may be signed by the appropriate department head in lieu of the SEO and the Qualified Investigator in lieu of the Senior Medical or Scientific Officer.

rules.rule_text: A Clinical Trial Site Information (CTSI) Form must be provided for each proposed clinical trial site, if known at the time of application. Even if not available at filing, it is required prior to commencement of the trial.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Clinical Trial Site Information (CTSI) Form should be provided for each proposed clinical trial site, if known at the time of the application as per C.05.005(c). Health Canada recognizes that not all information required in the CTSI form may be available at the time of filing a CTA. Sponsors are reminded that even if this information is not available when filing the CTA, it is required prior to commencement of the trial as per C.05.006(1)(d).

rules.rule_text: When referencing a Drug Master File (DMF), a letter of access from the DMF holder must be submitted, and the supporting DMF must have been submitted to and accepted by Health Canada prior to filing the CTA.
rules.verbatim_quote: The CTA sponsor should ensure that the supporting DMF (including submission of the letter of access and payment of related fees) has been submitted to and accepted by Health Canada prior to filing a CTA.

rules.rule_text: When referencing an application previously submitted by a different sponsor, a letter of access from that sponsor must be submitted, and the letter must include the file number and control number(s) of the referenced submission(s).
rules.verbatim_quote: The letter of access should include the file number and control number(s) of the referenced submission(s).
rules.exceptions: For pharmaceuticals where C&M info is referenced, sponsors must still complete the QOS template (Module 2, [2.3]).

rules.rule_text: Module 1 must include information regarding refusals by regulatory authorities outside Canada and foreign Research Ethics Boards, if applicable.
rules.verbatim_quote: Information regarding refusals by regulatory authorities outside Canada and foreign Research Ethics Boards, if applicable.

rules.rule_text: The Investigator's Brochure must be current, supplemented with up-to-date safety, nonclinical and clinical data, prepared in accordance with ICH E6, and reviewed at least annually and revised as necessary.
rules.verbatim_quote: A copy of the current Investigator's Brochure (IB), supplemented as appropriate with up-to-date safety, nonclinical and clinical data. The IB containing all information regarding the product to date should be prepared in accordance with the ICH E6, and should be reviewed at least annually and revised as necessary.
rules.exceptions: For products marketed in Canada, a reference to the most recent Canadian Product Monograph may be submitted if an updated IB is not available.

rules.rule_text: If the IB has been updated relative to a previously authorized CTA or CTA-A, a tabulated summary of changes must be provided, including a rationale for each change and any omissions or deletions from previous versions.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the IB has been updated relative to a version contained within a previously authorized CTA or CTA-A, a tabulated summary of the changes should be provided, including a rationale for each change that includes any omissions or deletions from previous versions if it has not been previously provided via a CTA-N.
rules.exceptions: Not required if changes were already communicated via CTA-N.

rules.rule_text: The CTA must include a Protocol Safety and Efficacy Assessment Template - Clinical Trial Application (PSEAT-CTA) as the Health Canada Summary in Module 1.4.1.
rules.verbatim_quote: Protocol Safety and Efficacy Assessment Template - Clinical Trial Application (PSEAT-CTA). A Protocol Synopsis in the format PSEAT-CTA.

rules.rule_text: A copy of the final proposed protocol(s), including version number, must be included in Module 1.7.1.
rules.verbatim_quote: A copy of the final proposed protocol(s), including version number.

rules.rule_text: Informed Consent Forms must include a statement regarding the risks and anticipated benefits and must be prepared in accordance with applicable laws governing consent, ICH E6, and the Tri-Council Policy Statement.
rules.verbatim_quote: A copy of the Informed Consent Forms (ICFs) to be used in conjunction with the clinical trial, including a statement regarding the risks and anticipated benefits to the clinical trial subjects as a result of their participation in the clinical trial. ICFs to be used in conjunction with the clinical trial should be prepared in accordance with applicable laws governing consent. The ICH E6 and the Tri-Council Policy Statement (TCPS) provides standards for the ICF.

rules.rule_text: The CTA must disclose any REB refusals, including the REB contact details, reasons for refusal, and the date of refusal, as per C.05.008(1)(c).
rules.verbatim_quote: The name, address and telephone number and, if applicable, the fax number and electronic mail address of any REB that has previously refused to approve the clinical trial protocol or amendment, its reasons for doing so and the date on which the refusal was given, if known at the time of submitting the application as per C.05.008(1)(c).

rules.rule_text: Module 2 (CTD Summaries) does not apply if the drug product has received NOC and/or DIN and has not been modified. In that case, sponsors should reference the control number of the prior application.
rules.verbatim_quote: This section does not apply if the drug product to be used in the clinical trial has received NOC and/or DIN and has not been modified. If the Quality information was previously submitted to, and authorized by Health Canada and has not changed, re-submission of the applicable Quality Summary may not be required. However, sponsors should refer to the control number of the prior application.
rules.exceptions: Only when quality information is unchanged and previously authorized.

rules.rule_text: For pharmaceuticals, a Quality Overall Summary (QOS) using the phase-appropriate QOS-CE template is required in Module 2.3.
rules.verbatim_quote: For Pharmaceuticals: a QOS is required [QOS-CE (CTA - Phase I)... QOS-CE (CTA - Phase II)... QOS-CE (CTA - Phase III)].

rules.rule_text: For placebo-controlled studies, a qualitative list of ingredients in the placebo must be submitted for pharmaceuticals; for biologics/radiopharmaceuticals, a description of the manufacturing process, qualitative and quantitative ingredient list, specifications, batches, stability, and facility information is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: For placebo-controlled studies, a qualitative list of the ingredients in the placebo should be submitted... For placebo-controlled studies, information on the placebo is also required including a description of the manufacturing process, a qualitative and quantitative list of ingredients, specifications, batches, stability and facility information.

rules.rule_text: Comparative bioavailability trial applications receive an administrative seven-day review target, but only when all qualifying criteria are met: single-dose studies in healthy adult volunteers, reference product marketed in CA/US/EU/AU/CH/JP, and maximum dose not exceeding reference product labelling.
rules.verbatim_quote: Health Canada's administrative seven (7) day review target applies to applications involving comparative bioavailability studies for pharmaceuticals only where: The studies are single-dose studies to be performed on healthy adult volunteers; The reference drug product is marketed in Canada, United States, European Union, Australia, Switzerland or Japan; and The maximum dose of the study drug does not exceed that specified in the labelling of the reference drug product.
rules.exceptions: Does not apply to biologics, radiopharmaceuticals, cellular therapies, xenografts, gene therapies, prophylactic vaccines, or reproductive/genetic technologies. Does not apply to comparative BA studies during drug development of new active substances.

rules.rule_text: CTA-As must be authorized by Health Canada prior to implementation of changes, except where immediate implementation is necessary to protect subject safety, in which case a CTA-A must be filed within 15 days after the change.
rules.verbatim_quote: CTA-As must be authorized by Health Canada prior to implementation of the changes [C.05.008]. However, if the sponsor is required to immediately make one or more of the amendments referred to in subsection (2) of C.05.008 because the clinical trial or the use of the drug for the purposes of the clinical trial endangers the health of clinical trial subjects or other persons, the sponsor may immediately make the amendment without prior review by Health Canada. Sponsors must notify Health Canada of this change, provide the relevant rationale in support of the immediate implementation and file a CTA-A that clearly identifies the change and the rationale for immediate implementation of the change within 15 days after the date of implementation of the amendment [C.05.008(4)].
rules.exceptions: Emergency safety amendments may be implemented immediately with 15-day retrospective filing.

rules.rule_text: A CTA-A is required when protocol amendments affect subject selection/assessment/dismissal, clinical efficacy evaluation, risk to subjects, safety evaluation, or treatment duration.
rules.verbatim_quote: As per section C.05.008(2), a CTA-A must be filed when the proposed amendments to the protocol: a) Affect the selection, assessment, or dismissal of a clinical trial subject; b) Affect the evaluation of the clinical efficacy of the drug; c) Alter the risk to the health of a clinical trial subject; d) Affect the safety evaluation of the drug; or e) Extend the duration of the treatment.

rules.rule_text: Amendments submitted when the CTA is under review will not be accepted. The sponsor must withdraw the active CTA and submit the amendment as a new CTA.
rules.verbatim_quote: Amendments submitted when the CTA is under review will not be accepted. Where a sponsor wishes to make changes to the CTA under review, the sponsor should withdraw the active CTA and submit the amendment as a new CTA.

rules.rule_text: For biologics and radiopharmaceuticals, manufacturing strategy changes that require new nonclinical and clinical data to support use are considered beyond the scope of a CTA-A and require a new CTA.
rules.verbatim_quote: for Biologics and Radiopharmaceuticals, differences in manufacturing strategies can lead to the production of a novel drug product requiring both non clinical and clinical data to support its use and are considered beyond the scope of an authorized CTA. In such cases, a new CTA is required.

rules.rule_text: All CTAs are subject to a 30-day default review period from the date of receipt of the completed application. If no objection is raised within 30 days, the trial may proceed (No Objection Letter issued).
rules.verbatim_quote: All CTAs, including those for comparative bioavailability studies, are subject to the 30 day default period from the date of receipt of the completed application as per C.05.005 or C.05.008.
rules.exceptions: Comparative BA studies targeted for 7-day review, but still subject to 30-day default.

rules.rule_text: Requests for Clarification during screening must be responded to within 2 calendar days.
rules.verbatim_quote: Requests for Clarification that are issued during screening should be responded to within 2 calendar days.

rules.rule_text: During review, sponsors must provide requested information within 2 calendar days. Failure to respond may result in submission withdrawal.
rules.verbatim_quote: The sponsor is responsible for resolving issues identified by Health Canada during the review process. Sponsors must provide the requested information within 2 calendar days [C.05.009]. Should the sponsor be unable to provide the requested information within the specified time frame, the submission may be withdrawn and resubmitted without prejudice.

rules.rule_text: CTA Notifications (CTA-N) must be filed within 15 calendar days of minor changes that do not meet CTA-A criteria. Changes may be implemented immediately.
rules.verbatim_quote: Notifications must be provided for changes to CTAs that do not meet the criteria for CTA-As. The changes may be implemented immediately, but Health Canada must be informed in writing, within 15 calendar days of the day of the change [C.05.007].

rules.rule_text: Prior to initiating a clinical trial at a site, the sponsor must ensure the REB Attestation and Qualified Investigator Undertaking forms are completed and kept on file, and the CTSI form has been filed with Health Canada.
rules.verbatim_quote: Prior to initiating a clinical trial or implementing an amendment to a clinical trial at a site, the sponsor must ensure that the REB Attestation and Qualified Investigator Undertaking forms have been completed and kept on file by the sponsor, and that the Clinical Trial Site Information form has been filed with Health Canada.

rules.rule_text: The proposed trial protocol and ICF must be reviewed and approved by a Research Ethics Board (REB) prior to initiating the trial or implementing an amendment at each site.
rules.verbatim_quote: Prior to initiating a clinical trial or implementing an amendment to a clinical trial at a site, the proposed trial protocol and ICF must be reviewed and approved by a REB as defined in the Regulations.

rules.rule_text: There must be no more than one qualified investigator at each clinical trial site. The qualified investigator must be a physician (or dentist for dental trials) and member in good standing of a professional medical or dental association.
rules.verbatim_quote: There must be no more than one (1) qualified investigator at each site. This restriction does not apply to sub-investigators.

rules.rule_text: Prior to commencement, sponsors must complete and submit a CTSI form for each clinical trial site.
rules.verbatim_quote: Prior to commencement of the clinical trial or implementation of a CTA-A, sponsors are required to complete and submit a Clinical Trial Site Information (CTSI) form for each clinical trial site [C.05.006(1)(d)/C.05.008(1)(c)].
rules.exceptions: Ancillary locations for X-rays, MRIs, or blood collections do not require CTSI forms.

rules.rule_text: All investigational biologic drug product lots must undergo lot release per the Guidance for Sponsors: Lot Release Program for Schedule D (Biologic) Drugs before use in clinical trials.
rules.verbatim_quote: All investigational biologic drug product lots to be used in a clinical trial are subject to the Lot Release requirements as outlined in Guidance for Sponsors: Lot Release Program for Schedule D (Biologic) Drugs.
rules.exceptions: Prophylactic vaccines have a separate lot release process requiring testing protocols and/or Certificates of Analysis.
cross_references: Lot Release Program for Schedule D

rules.rule_text: For importation of clinical trial drugs, a No Objection Letter must be provided at the time of importation to facilitate shipment and demonstrate compliance.
rules.verbatim_quote: For sponsors who wish to import a drug into Canada for the purpose of a clinical trial, a No Objection Letter should be provided at the time of importation to facilitate shipment and to demonstrate compliance with section C.05.006 or section C.05.008 of the Regulations.

rules.rule_text: The sponsor bears ultimate responsibility for the correct handling and storage of imported clinical trial drugs, regardless of any delegation agreements with importers.
rules.verbatim_quote: Regardless of the agreements in place for the importation of the product, the sponsor ultimately bears the responsibility for the correct handling and storage of the product to be used in the clinical trial.

rules.rule_text: In the event of premature trial discontinuation, Health Canada must be notified no later than 15 calendar days after the date of discontinuance, including detailed reasons, impact on ongoing Canadian trials, confirmation that investigators have been notified, and confirmation that drug sale/import has stopped.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the event of the premature discontinuation of a trial, in its entirety, or at a clinical trial site, for which a CTA or CTA-A has been filed in Canada, the responsible Directorate must be notified as soon as possible, but no later than 15 calendar days after the date of discontinuance [C.05.015].

rules.rule_text: Notification of premature discontinuation of a clinical trial outside Canada must also be submitted to Health Canada if the same drug has ongoing trials in Canada and the discontinuation was for safety reasons.
rules.verbatim_quote: Notification of a premature discontinuation of a clinical trial or clinical trial site outside Canada, for which there are ongoing trials with the drug in Canada, should also be submitted to the appropriate Directorate if such discontinuation was carried out for safety reasons.
rules.exceptions: Only required when discontinuation is safety-related.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors may only resume a discontinued trial when a No Objection Letter has been issued from the appropriate Directorate or when the 30-day default period has passed without objection.
rules.verbatim_quote: The sponsors may only resume the trial when a No Objection Letter (NOL) has been issued from the appropriate Directorate or when the 30 day default period has passed without an objection.

rules.rule_text: During a clinical trial, serious unexpected ADRs that are neither fatal nor life-threatening must be reported to Health Canada within 15 days of awareness. Fatal or life-threatening ADRs must be reported within 7 days, with a complete follow-up report within 8 days thereafter.
rules.verbatim_quote: During a clinical trial the sponsor is required to inform Health Canada, in an expedited manner, of any serious unexpected adverse drug reaction, in respect of the drug that has occurred inside or outside Canada [C.05.014]: a) Where it is neither fatal nor life-threatening, within 15 days after becoming aware of the information; b) Where it is fatal or life-threatening, within 7 days after becoming aware of the information. Within 8 days after having initially informed Health Canada of the fatal or life-threatening ADR, submit as complete a report as possible.

rules.rule_text: ADR expedited reports must meet all three criteria: serious, unexpected, and suspected causal relationship. Reports must be submitted individually per ICH E2A data elements, using ADR Expedited Reporting Summary Form (Form 01-03) and CIOMS Form.
rules.verbatim_quote: Each ADR which is subject to expedited reporting to Health Canada should be reported individually in accordance with the data element(s) specified in the Health Canada/ICH Guidance Document E2A... Expedited reports are required for events that meet all of these three criteria: serious, unexpected and a suspected causal relationship.
rules.exceptions: Adverse reactions considered unrelated by both the investigator and the sponsor should not be reported.

rules.rule_text: When a fatal or serious outcome is the primary efficacy endpoint, the protocol should clearly indicate the serious event(s) that will be treated as disease-related and not subject to expedited reporting.
rules.verbatim_quote: When a fatal or other serious outcome is the primary efficacy endpoint in a clinical trial, the protocol should clearly indicate the serious event(s) that will be treated as disease-related and not subject to expedited reporting.

rules.rule_text: The Investigator's Brochure must be reviewed at least annually and revised as necessary. Updated IBs must be submitted separately as a CTA-N with a statement confirming that protocols and ICFs of ongoing trials do not require changes, accompanied by a list of changes with rationale.
rules.verbatim_quote: the IB, including all safety information and global status, should be reviewed at least annually and revised as necessary... the updated IB should be submitted separately as a CTA-N, and include a statement confirming that the protocol and/or ICF of all ongoing trials do not require changes as a result of the updated IB. In all cases, the updated IB should be accompanied by a list of changes that clearly describes the sections that have changed, including a rationale for each change.
rules.exceptions: If a CTA or CTA-A is being submitted, the updated IB should accompany the application instead.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must retain all clinical trial records for a period of 15 years. Records must be made available to Health Canada within 2 days of a safety-related request, or within 7 days for any other request.
rules.verbatim_quote: The sponsor shall maintain all records referred to in the applicable Regulations for a period of 15 years. Records must be made available to the relevant Directorate within 2 days of a request if there is a concern regarding the use of the drug... and a risk to health of the subjects involved in that trial. In any other case, records must be provided within 7 days of a request [C.05.013].

rules.rule_text: Clinical trial drug labels must be in both official languages (English and French) and must include: a statement that the drug is investigational for use only by a qualified investigator, name/number/identifying mark, expiration date, storage conditions, lot number, sponsor name and address, and protocol code.
rules.verbatim_quote: the sponsor shall ensure that the drug bears a label that sets out the following information in both official languages: (a) a statement indicating that the drug is an investigational drug to be used only by a qualified investigator; (b) the name, number or identifying mark of the drug; (c) the expiration date of the drug; (d) the recommended storage conditions for the drug; (e) the lot number of the drug; (f) the name and address of the sponsor; (g) the protocol code or identification
rules.exceptions: Labels should not be submitted to Health Canada unless requested.

rules.rule_text: For CTA-A Clinical amendments, the sponsor must provide a copy of the amended or working protocol with a clear description of changes (original vs. revised wording), a rationale for each proposed change, and a copy of the most recently authorized protocol including version number.
rules.verbatim_quote: A copy of the amended or working protocol with a clear description of the changes that are being proposed (that is, original wording vs. revised wording), a rationale for each proposed change, and a copy of the most recently authorized protocol, including version number.
rules.exceptions: Cross-referencing the protocol is not acceptable; changes must be listed separately or annotated.

rules.rule_text: The pre-CTA consultation meeting record must be prepared by the sponsor and sent to the appropriate Directorate within 14 days of the consultation. A copy approved by all parties must be included in the subsequent CTA.
rules.verbatim_quote: The sponsor should prepare and send to the appropriate Directorate a written record of the discussions and conclusions of the consultation meeting within 14 days of the consultation date... A copy of the record of discussions and conclusions approved by all parties at attendance at the meeting should be included in the subsequent CTA.
rules.exceptions: Only applicable if a pre-CTA consultation meeting was held.
